estimand: Candidate-seat decisive-game win share of v0.7s search versus v0.6 under the accepted round-1 draft and CONDITIONAL_SETUP_V1=all. The formal family is thirteen qualification cells by two candidate seats. Four ranked-taxonomy cells independently condition each candidate seat on at least one enabled trait member while leaving the opponent naturally distributed; eight fixed-template cells are supplemental tactical stress panels, not literal ranked offer samples. Draws and Armageddon are reported separately.

off.parityNote: Exact v0.7s-versus-v0.7 search-off symmetry control; no search audit rows are permitted.

uncapped.parityNote: Uncapped search is an exploratory upper bound and is not a live-latency qualification profile.

server_300.parityNote: Lower-bound emulation of the server's 300ms outer per-match circuit. The sim circuit excludes wrapper and driver-construction overhead, so this is not exact live parity.

conservative_200_275.parityNote: Conservative qualification profile with incumbent fallback at 200ms and a 275ms per-match circuit; deliberately stricter than the current server and not an exact-live claim.

cells.purpose: Byte-policy alias symmetry control under the current ranked draft and setup composition.
Exploratory upper bound on search strength under the current ranked composition distribution.

Primary current-ranked qualification cell under the conservative latency profile.
Seat-balanced ranked cohort where the candidate physical army contains at least one enabled MAGIC attacker; the opponent composition is unrestricted.
Seat-balanced ranked cohort where the candidate physical army contains at least one enabled MELEE_MAGIC attacker; the opponent composition is unrestricted.
Seat-balanced ranked cohort where the candidate physical army contains at least one enabled creature with a non-null aura effect; the opponent composition is unrestricted.
Seat-balanced ranked cohort where the candidate physical army contains at least one enabled RANGE attacker; the opponent composition is unrestricted.

estimatedRuntime.m4Max: Approximately 3-6 hours at concurrency 12, run one immutable cell at a time after the outcome-blind taxonomy-plan preflight.
estimatedRuntime.zinc: Approximately 8-16 hours when the host is otherwise free; the host/runtime envelope cannot be mixed with M4 cells.
estimatedRuntime.basis: The prior M4 ladder completed uncapped 4,000-game search cells in roughly 15-19 minutes at 16 oversubscribed workers. This estimate adds a 12-worker margin plus 8,000 taxonomy games and deterministic rejection setup work. No combat seed may be spent for calibration.

serverReference.explanation: The server source pins the accepted round-1 draft and CONDITIONAL_SETUP_V1=all, but its lockfile resolves common at ded20c9 before the v0.7s measurement alias/harness. Its 300ms breaker is an outer wall-clock circuit around per-decision driver construction; server_300 is only the closest lower-bound simulation emulation. No report may call this byte-identical live runtime.

seedAudit.derivation: For logical ordinal o, seed=(716560508 + imul(o,3534407539)) mod 2^32; the odd multiplier is an affine uint32 bijection. Corpus-colliding slots use the preregistered spare ordinals in ordinalOverrides; no statistical-independence claim is made.
seedAudit.logicalSlotLayout: Each registered scenario owns a disjoint contiguous ordinal block: fixed-side-swap cells reserve scenario/setup/combat slots, while taxonomy cells reserve scenario, 128 setup proposals and combat separately for each candidate seat.
seedAudit.declaration: Outcome-blind preregistration finalized from byte-exact v9d same-cutoff corpus replays before any registered combat outcome. The guard sealBefore was reset to 2026-07-17T09:15:00Z for the fresh final-protocol v9d lineage at initial observation 2026-07-16T09:14:44.280Z, before either registered corpus scan, after the v7 through v9c lineages were abandoned; it will not move again. The user-reported 100k outcome is retained only to recover its seed schedule and is not execution evidence for this ladder.
